title: pavetta parasitica
intro: Pavetta parasitica, also known as the "Strychnine Tree" or "Yellow Wood," is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant contains strychnine, a toxic alkaloid that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pavetta parasitica include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use requires caution due to the presence of toxic compounds.
